title: Day one, again
blurb: A relapse wasn't the end of recovery — it was part of it.
lead: A story about the shame of relapse, and the courage it takes to reach out and begin again.

blocks.text: The streak had felt like an identity. Ninety days. Then one hard night, and it was gone. The shame afterward was worse than the craving — a voice saying you were a fraud, a failure, that you'd ruined everything and might as well keep going.

That voice nearly won. What interrupted it was a single, hard phone call — telling someone in a recovery group the truth. No judgement came back. Just: 'Welcome back. Day one is still worth showing up for.'
Relapse, it turned out, wasn't proof that recovery had failed. It was information — about a trigger, an unmet need, a gap in the plan. Recovery continued, a little wiser.
The streak restarted. But something had changed: the goal was no longer a perfect record. It was staying honest, and staying connected.
The bravest thing I ever did wasn't staying sober. It was picking up the phone the day after I wasn't.
This is an illustrative composite — a realistic story drawn from common experiences, not a real named individual. It's here to remind you that change is possible.
